tone_and_voice: Participatory and dialogue-oriented -- distinct from Citizen Feedback's broader municipal framing, Community Scorecard writing must reflect the methodology's specific joint community-provider accountability purpose, framing findings as agenda items for a facility-community dialogue, not a standalone verdict.

section_rules.executive_summary: Must frame findings explicitly as scorecard inputs for the next facility-community dialogue session, not as a final judgment -- this is a structural feature of the scorecard methodology and should be stated plainly.
section_rules.discussion: Should explain the scorecard methodology's dual-sided purpose (crediting specific improvements while separately documenting specific gaps) as intentional design, not as an inconsistent set of findings.
section_rules.methodology: State the specific facility/service scored and the participatory scorecard process used (e.g. community scoring session, facility management joint review), since this differs from a standard survey methodology.
section_rules.limitations: Note participation levels by location if uneven, since scorecard validity depends on adequate, representative community participation at each site.
section_rules.recommendations: Every recommendation should be framed as a specific agenda item for the next facility-community dialogue meeting, with a named responsible party (community, facility, or both jointly).
section_rules.policy_implications: Rare -- most findings are facility-level; escalate only if a finding is consistent across multiple facilities in the same scorecard exercise.
section_rules.lessons_learned: Reflect on the value of the scorecard's structured, dual-sided format relative to a single blended satisfaction rating.
section_rules.conclusion: Must name the specific standing agenda items for the next facility-community dialogue -- a scorecard report without a clear next-meeting agenda has not fulfilled its methodological purpose.
section_rules.annexes: Include the specific scorecard indicators/questions used in the participatory session, since these are typically co-developed with the community and facility, not a standard fixed instrument.
section_rules.evidence_style: Present findings as scorecard items (specific, dialogue-ready) rather than as narrative prose alone -- this report type benefits from a more structured, itemized evidence presentation than a standard survey report.

common_kpis: gender participation balance (particularly relevant for health facility scorecards)
common_risks: uneven participation across scorecard locations undermining comparability
treating scorecard findings as final rather than dialogue inputs
facility management not preparing a response ahead of the community dialogue session
typical_recommendations: present findings at the next facility-community dialogue with named agenda items
facility-level transparency measures (budget noticeboards, verbal briefings)
verify participation balance across scorecard locations for future rounds
sector_knowledge.typical_donor_expectations: Donors funding participatory accountability programmes expect scorecard reports to demonstrate the joint community-provider dialogue process explicitly, not just present findings as a one-way assessment.

forbidden_behaviors: Never present community scorecard findings as a final, one-way judgment rather than as input to a joint facility-community dialogue process.
Never omit the specific next-dialogue agenda framing that distinguishes this report type from a standard citizen survey.
Never generalize a single facility's scorecard finding to a broader system-level conclusion without evidence of consistency across multiple facilities.
